An embassy spokesperson and a U.K. Foreign Office spokesperson sought to distance the government from the leaked remarks, telling Fox News Digital: "These were private, informal comments made to a group of U.K. sixth-form students visiting the U.S. in early February. They are certainly not any reflection of the U.K. government’s position."

The wide-ranging informal discussion, the spokesperson explained, focused on diplomacy and the political issues of the day that students asked questions about, stressing that the remarks were clearly never intended as on-record statements of government policy.
PRINCE WILLIAM VIEWS TRUMP RELATIONSHIP AS KEY TO MONARCHY'S FUTURE: EXPERT
President Donald Trump speaks with Britain's King Charles III during a state dinner in the East Room of the White House in Washington, D.C., April 28, 2026. (Aaron Schwartz/AFP)

Still, the controversy raises broader questions that extend beyond diplomatic optics: whether the symbolic "special relationship" between Washington and London has been eclipsed by more immediate U.S. strategic priorities, particularly Israel’s central role in American Middle East security calculations.
Barak Seener, senior fellow at the London-based Henry Jackson Society, said Turner’s remarks reflected a hard strategic reality rather than a diplomatic blunder.

"Ambassador Christian Turner was giving a realistic assessment that echoed President Trump’s criticism of NATO, calling it a ‘paper tiger’ for not contributing to joint U.S.-Israel-led operations against Iran," Seener told Fox News Digital.

Seener pointed to his recent report, "Israel 2048: A Blueprint for an Asymmetric Geopolitical Power," arguing that Israel’s expanding military capabilities increasingly function as a force multiplier for U.S. regional interests. By contrast, Seener argued, Britain’s modern strategic value has weakened despite its historic rhetoric.

"King Charles, in his recent speech to the U.S. Congress, was forced to emphasise the U.K. and U.S.’s shared culture and history rather than recent military contributions simply because the U.K.’s naval and military capabilities have been completely hollowed out," Seener said.
CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP
King Charles made history as the second British monarch to address Congress, after his mother, the late Queen Elizabeth, first did so in 1991. (Henry Nicholls/POOL/AFP via Getty Images)
He added that Charles’ invocation of past joint sacrifices "does not bear any relevance to today" given Britain’s reported refusal to allow U.S. use of Royal Air Force bases for strikes on Iran.

The White House, however, emphasized continuity over controversy, telling Fox News Digital that, "President Trump has enjoyed welcoming Their Majesties to the White House this week, including yesterday morning, when he highlighted the historic, special relationship between the United States and the United Kingdom," White House spokesperson Anna Kelly said.
"The events of this visit are unprecedented in scope and spectacle, and the President enjoyed hosting a beautiful State Dinner yesterday evening," she added.
Efrat Lachter is a foreign correspondent for Fox News Digital covering international affairs and the United Nations. Follow her on X @efratlachter. Stories can be sent to efrat.lachter@fox.com.

title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism
summary: Two men were stabbed in north London's Barnet borough, prompting a counterterrorism-led investigation into a potentially antisemitic incident.
story: Police remain on scene in London as authorities investigate a suspected antisemitic stabbing that injured two people. A suspect is in custody. (Credit: APTN)

Two people were stabbed in north London on Wednesday in an attack that police have now formally declared a terrorist incident, prompting a major emergency response and an ongoing counterterrorism investigation.

Assistant Commissioner Laurence Taylor, the Metropolitan Police’s counterterrorism chief, said the attack has been officially classified as terrorism as investigators work to determine the motive and whether the Jewish community was deliberately targeted.

Officers were called to Highfield Avenue in the Barnet borough at about 11:16 a.m. following reports of multiple stabbings, according to the Metropolitan Police. Local and armed officers responded alongside the London Ambulance Service.
A 45-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder and remains in custody, police said. Authorities are working to determine his nationality and background.
Two men, ages 76 and 34, were treated at the scene for stab wounds before being taken to a hospital, where they remain and are "being looked after," Taylor said.
SYNAGOGUE IN LONDON TARGETED IN ATTEMPTED 'ANTISEMITIC HATE CRIME,' UK POLICE SAY

A police officer at the scene where two people were stabbed on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, in a London neighborhood with a large Jewish community. A 45-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder over what authorities called an antisemitic attack. (Lucy North/PA via AP)
Police said the suspect also attacked responding officers before he was subdued with a Taser. No officers were injured.
Counterterrorism officers are leading the investigation, working with security services to establish the full circumstances and develop a complete intelligence picture, Taylor said.
"Whilst I must stress this investigation is at an early stage, we are working quickly to understand exactly what happened," Counter Terrorism Policing head Laurence Taylor said.
NYC POLICE HUNT SUSPECT ACCUSED OF STABBING JEWISH MAN IN CHEST WHILE MAKING ANTISEMITIC STATEMENTS

The stabbing unfolded in the Barnet area, near Golders Green, which is known for its large Jewish community. Authorities have indicated the case is being treated as a potentially antisemitic incident, though motive has not been confirmed.

The stabbing unfolded in the Barnet area, near Golders Green, which is known for its large Jewish community, and police said one line of inquiry is whether the attack deliberately targeted London’s Jewish community, though a motive has not been confirmed.

A statement posted on X by Shomrim, a volunteer neighborhood watch group in Orthodox Jewish communities, said a man was seen "armed with a knife" on Golders Green Road and was detained by members before police arrived. The group claimed the suspect attempted to target Jewish members of the public — a detail police have not independently confirmed.
GUILTY VERDICTS IN ISIS PLOT POLICE SAY COULD HAVE BEEN 'THE DEADLIEST TERRORIST ATTACK IN UK HISTORY'
Shomrim said two victims were treated by Hatzola, a volunteer emergency medical service.

Police at the scene where two people were stabbed on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, in a London neighborhood with a large Jewish community. A 45-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder over what authorities called an antisemitic attack. (Lucy North/PA via AP)

Detective Chief Superintendent Luke Williams said officers "swiftly Tasered and arrested the suspect before he could cause further harm," adding that investigators are "considering all possible motives" and will maintain a visible police presence in the area.
ANTISEMITISM WATCHDOG SLAMS UK GOVERNMENT RESPONSE TO 70K RALLY AGAINST JEW-HATRED
Williams said police are "aware of the significant distress and concern this incident is likely to cause," and will remain in the area to carry out inquiries and reassure residents.
The attack comes amid heightened concern over antisemitic incidents across the United Kingdom. Authorities are examining recent incidents in London but have not established any direct connection.
‘ACT OF VIOLENCE’: NEW JERSEY RABBI INJURED IN SUSPECTED HATE CRIME BEFORE JEWISH HOLIDAY

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the violence, calling attacks on Jewish residents "an attack on Britain," while London Mayor Sadiq Khan said there is "no place for antisemitism" in the city.

Conservative MP Kemi Badenoch said, "Jewish people in our country are under constant attack. This is no longer a growing pattern. There is an epidemic of violence against Jewish people. It is now a national emergency and needs to be treated as such by the Government and public authorities."
Dov Forman, a Golders Green resident and Holocaust educator, described the attack as part of what he sees as a broader and deeply concerning trend.
UK STABBING, VEHICLE ATTACK INJURES 4 OUTSIDE SYNAGOGUE, POLICE SAY

Police officers work by cordon at the junction of Golders Green Road and the North Circular Road, in the Golders Green neighborhood of north London, on April 29, 2026. (JUSTIN TALLIS / AFP via Getty Images)

"Yet again, terror has been brought to our doorstep here in Golders Green, in the heart of London’s Jewish community," Forman said. "Earlier today, two visibly Jewish men were stabbed in what is being described as an antisemitic attack. For many, this is not being seen as an isolated act of violence, but as part of a broader and deeply troubling pattern. There is growing concern that extemist rhetoric and Islamist extremism, including calls to ‘globalize the intifada,’ has helped fuel an environment in which hatred against Jews is increasingly normalized, unchecked, and dangerously emboldened."
Israeli President Isaac Herzog said he was "horrified by yet another violent attack on Jews in broad daylight on the streets of London" and called for urgent action.
"No Jew anywhere in the world should be a target because of their faith," Herzog said, adding that authorities must act "before the next antisemitic attack occurs."
Authorities urged anyone with information to contact police as the investigation continues.
CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP

The number of antisemitic incidents reported across the U.K. has soared since the Oct. 7, 2023, attack by Hamas on Israel and the subsequent Gaza war, according to the Community Security Trust. The group recorded 3,700 incidents in 2025, up from 1,662 in 2022.
The Associated Press contributed to this report.
Bradford Betz is a Fox News Digital breaking reporter covering crime, political issues, and much more.

title: How the Lively-Baldoni settlement came together
summary: Behind the scenes, talks to resolve the Justin Baldoni-Blake Lively case began in earnest last month, after a judge dismissed most of Lively’s claims, a source familiar with the matter told NBC News.
story: With two weeks to go before a scheduled trial, “It Ends With Us” co-stars Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni announced a surprise settlement Monday, ending a dispute that had played out in court hearings and headlines for almost a year and a half.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
Publicly, lawyers for the former co-stars continued to hash out key pretrial issues before a federal judge.

But behind the scenes, talks to resolve the case began in earnest last month, after a judge dismissed most of Lively’s claims, a source familiar with the matter told NBC News. The teams held meetings over the weekend and finalized the deal Monday, the source said. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.

By Monday evening, after attorneys for Lively and Baldoni issued a joint statement, Lively stepped out in full glam for the Met Gala, where she smiled and waved to fans as she made her way up the steps of the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City.

The appearance marked an unofficial close of a chapter for Lively, whose personal life (including her relationship with husband Ryan Reynolds and friendship with Taylor Swift) became a public fascination amid the ongoing Baldoni dispute.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

In December 2024, Lively alleged in a sexual harassment complaint that Baldoni repeatedly harassed her on the set and crossed boundaries during intimate scenes in the film, which had been released in August of that year.

The movie, which was based on the novel by Colleen Hoover, follows Lily Bloom (played by Lively) and her abusive relationship with a neurosurgeon named Ryle Kincaid (played by Baldoni). Baldoni optioned the film in 2019 with Wayfarer Studios and later signed on to direct and star in it. Lively also was a producer.
Lively also alleged that Wayfarer Studios retaliated against her after she complained about allegations of misconduct on the set.
Baldoni and Wayfarer denied all of Lively's claims.

In January 2025, Baldoni countersued Lively and Reynolds, alleging defamation and extortion. He argued that they tried to wreck his reputation and accused Lively of using her grievances as a way to “seize control” of the movie, which was co-produced by Wayfarer and the Sony-owned Columbia Pictures.
Baldoni also lodged a defamation suit against The New York Times over an article headlined “‘We Can Bury Anyone’: Inside a Hollywood Smear Machine.”
Liman tossed out both suits last year.
Lively and Baldoni reach settlement in yearslong case
The stars' legal saga continued, however, with a trial set for May 18.

Lively's suit centered on 13 claims, including sexual harassment in violation of Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, a landmark law that bars employment discrimination based on gender and other grounds. She sought unspecified compensatory and punitive damages.

Last month, U.S. District Judge Lewis J. Liman tossed out 10 of Lively's claims against Baldoni, including harassment, defamation and conspiracy. Liman let three other claims stand: breach of contract, retaliation and aiding and abetting in retaliation.

Later in the month, the actors’ legal teams debated expert witness testimony before Liman. He then asked both sides to look into the availability of some of the expert witnesses they planned to call at trial to participate in a pretrial hearing.
That hearing had been scheduled for Friday.

In their joint statement Monday, Bryan Freedman, Ellyn Garofalo, Michael Gottlieb and Esra Hudson, attorneys for Baldoni and Lively, said: "The end product — the movie ‘It Ends With Us’ — is a source of pride to all of us who worked to bring it to life. Raising awareness, and making a meaningful impact in the lives of domestic violence survivors — and all survivors — is a goal that we stand behind.”

title: OpenAI co-founder grilled at Musk trial: 'You just happen to be $30 billion richer?'
summary: OpenAI co-founder and President Greg Brockman disclosed in a trial Monday that his stake in the firm is worth nearly $30 billion.
story: OAKLAND, Calif. — OpenAI co-founder and President Greg Brockman disclosed in a trial Monday that his stake in the firm is worth nearly $30 billion, confirming a figure that co-founder Elon Musk has pointed to in arguing that OpenAI has abandoned its mission as a nonprofit organization.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

Brockman took the stand during the fourth day of the trial. Musk is suing Brockman and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, alleging that they unlawfully converted a charity that he helped start into a for-profit business, best known for creating the popular artificial intelligence app ChatGPT.

Brockman was repeatedly asked to reconcile his nearly $30 billion stake with OpenAI’s stated mission of making AI technology to benefit all of humanity. He testified that the mission hasn’t changed, even after its board has sold equity stakes to outside investors.
“You just happen to be $30 billion richer?” Musk lawyer Steven Molo asked.
“Compensation was certainly secondary to the mission,” Brockman responded.
The trial could reshape OpenAI as an organization and alter the industrywide push for advanced AI. It’s also unusual for featuring testimony from rival tech billionaires.

Musk, the CEO of Tesla, SpaceX and xAI, vowed to make Brockman and Altman “the most hated men in America” through evidence presented at the trial, according to the content of text messages included in a court filing Sunday. Musk texted Brockman two days before the trial began to gauge the possibility of a settlement, according to the filing by OpenAI’s lawyers.

Monday’s testimony was combative, raising ideas about what kind of tech industry wealth is truly deserved. Molo at one point compared Brockman to a “guy who robs a bank,” a phrase that U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers struck as argumentative. At another point, Molo asked Brockman about transferring assets from OpenAI’s charitable side to its for-profit side to create a “money-making machine.”

Brockman pushed back. He testified that he was given his stake in OpenAI in 2018, years before the release of ChatGPT, when it was far from certain that the organization would be successful either financially or technologically. He said that OpenAI’s board granted him the stake and that he didn’t participate in the board vote to do so.

He testified that OpenAI is still controlled by a nonprofit foundation and that its for-profit arm is what’s known as a public-benefit corporation, a type of corporation that must take into account both a public-spirited mission and the interests of shareholders. He said that all of OpenAI’s co-founders, Musk included, wanted it to have a for-profit arm of some kind and that what they disagreed on was the details.
And he said Musk has little connection to OpenAI as it exists now. Musk stopped donating to OpenAI in 2017 and left its board in 2018.
“That is something that we’ve built through blood, sweat and tears, during all these years since Elon left,” Brockman testified.
The first OpenAI office was Brockman’s apartment in San Francisco, he testified.

Brockman is relatively obscure compared with the trial’s other billionaires, Musk and Altman, but he is a well-known figure within the tech industry. Before he co-founded OpenAI in 2015, he was the chief technology officer of the payments company Stripe.

But his passion for the subjects at issue came through. Brockman at times spoke so quickly that the court reporter making a transcript couldn’t keep up, and the judge stepped in to urge him to speak more slowly. Several times, Brockman had to repeat himself to make the record clear.
OpenAI said in March that it was valued at $852 billion after its latest funding round. After a restructuring in October, the nonprofit foundation arm owned 26%, while OpenAI employees owned 26%.

Molo, though, kept turning the testimony back to the nearly $30 billion figure, which Musk posted about last year on X. Musk’s lawsuit alleges that Brockman, Altman and others breached their duties to OpenAI as a charitable organization and unlawfully enriched themselves.
Molo mentioned the nearly $30 billion figure more than a dozen times during more than two hours of questioning Brockman.
“Do you believe that your nearly $30 billion stake ... breaches your duty to humanity?” he asked.
“No, I believe that we have developed the most well-capitalized nonprofit in human history,” Brockman responded.

Molo pressed Brockman about whether his primary motive was actually financial. He quoted from a September 2017 journal entry in which Brockman wrote to himself, as he pondered OpenAI’s future, “Financially, what will take me to $1B?”
Brockman testified that the money was always secondary.
Molo asked Brockman whether he would consider limiting his own compensation to $1 billion and giving the balance of his wealth back to the nonprofit arm of OpenAI.
“Do you think, sitting here today, given that you’re good with the $1 billion, do you think you should give the $29 billion back to the charity?” the lawyer asked.
“That’s not how I think about it,” Brockman responded. “There are assumptions baked into the question,” he added, without elaborating.
At another point, Molo asked, “It takes $30 billion to get you out of bed in the morning, but $1 billion doesn’t get you out of bed in the morning?”
Brockman responded, “That’s not what I’m saying.”
Not mentioned during Monday’s testimony was Musk’s net worth, estimated at $657 billion, the highest in the world, according to Bloomberg.

title: Spirit Airlines doomed by high fuel prices due to 'recent geopolitical events,' court docs state
summary: Spirit Airlines said in court papers filed Monday that it was forced to ground its fleet for good over the weekend because "recent geopolitical events resulted in a massive and sustained increase in fuel prices."While the court papers did not specify the nature of those geopolitical events, jet fuel prices have soared in the two months since the start of the war in Iran."The Debtors and their advisors searched for increased capital and any sources of savings or liquidity, leaving no option unexp
story: Spirit Airlines said in court papers filed Monday that it was forced to ground its fleet for good over the weekend because "recent geopolitical events resulted in a massive and sustained increase in fuel prices."
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.
While the court papers did not specify the nature of those geopolitical events, jet fuel prices have soared in the two months since the start of the war in Iran.

"The Debtors and their advisors searched for increased capital and any sources of savings or liquidity, leaving no option unexplored," the airline stated in records filed Monday in federal bankruptcy court in the Southern District of New York.
"It became clear on Thursday that sufficient incremental liquidity would not be found, and that there were no longer any viable paths to a restructuring or continued operations."

But Spirit waited until early Saturday to pull the plug on its operations, stranding thousands of travelers midtrip and forcing countless others to rebook on other airlines. Many passengers said they received no warning that Spirit was going under.
Spirit, in the court papers, said it asked the Federal Aviation Administration "to issue a ground stop for Spirit flights to ensure no accidental dispatches" at 3 a.m. Saturday.

Why then? Spirit Airlines' debtors decided "the safest time to make this announcement was when no planes were in the air and all crew who were away from their bases had sufficient time to check into hotel accommodations," the court papers state.
"These actions were taken to prioritize the safety of passengers and crew," they state.
Now, Spirit is requesting the authority to "take necessary steps to wind down its fleet operations."

"In particular, Spirit is hereby requesting authorization to abandon the aircraft that it owns and are not already subject to a purchase agreement, and to either sell or abandon its owned spare engines and spare parts," the papers state.

In its official announcement, Spirit said it will automatically refund tickets purchased directly by flyers with a credit or debit card, while those who booked through third parties would have to contact their travel agent.
Spirit said compensation for customers who used vouchers, credits or loyalty points would be determined later as part of the bankruptcy process.
Meanwhile, some 17,000 Spirit workers are now without jobs, including 5,500 flight attendants.

One of five budget airlines in the U.S., Spirit had struggled for years to stay profitable and twice declared bankruptcy, even as it paid its top executives millions of dollars in salaries and bonuses.
In the first two months of 2026, Spirit lost $60 million — and that was before President Donald Trump began the current conflict with Iran.
Spirit Airlines attempted to be acquired by JetBlue several years ago. But a federal judge sided with Biden-era Justice Department officials that such a combination tripped antitrust rules.
On Friday, NBC News reported that discussions between the Trump administration and Spirit Airlines to save the company were going nowhere and that the carrier could shut down on Saturday.

Earlier, Trump said the White House had given Spirit Airlines “a final proposal” for a federal bailout to save it. But Trump also warned that he was reluctant to risk taxpayer funds on a business that has already been through two bankruptcies.

title: The 2026 Met Gala Guest List: Here Are All the Stars Who Attended
summary: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here
story: It's the first Monday in May and that means fashion’s biggest night out is officially here.
The 2026 Met Gala is expected, per usual, to be a star-studded and A-list affair, with some of the biggest and most impressive names in music, movies, sports, fashion and more in attendance.
Follow live coverage

The guest list was shared on Monday afternoon with the media lining up at the red carpet. This came after a slew of stars were already revealed to be attending as the gala’s co-chairs and committee members for 2026.
This year’s co-chairs Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Nicole Kidman, as well as Anna Wintour, now in her new role as Condé Nast’s chief content officer.
Kidman and Williams attended the 2025 Met Gala, but this will be Beyoncé's first appearance in a decade.

The four chairs will be joined by Anthony Vaccarello and Zoë Kravitz, who will also co-chair the celebration's host committee that includes additional celebrities who will undoubtedly be dressed to the nines in designer wear.

The 2026 Met Gala theme is “Costume Art,” with the dress code described as “fashion is art.” Beyoncé, Kidman, Williams and the rest of the VIP guests are expected to interpret the theme and dress code by expressing “their own relationship to fashion as an embodied artform,” according to Vogue’s guidelines.

At last year's Met Gala, Rihanna surprised guests, fans and Met Gala viewers by revealing her pregnancy, André 3000 literally carried a piano on his back, and Bad Bunny honored his Puerto Rican roots, while Zendaya and Anna Sawai had nearly identical looks.
Expect many jaw-dropping, head-turning, inspirational and, perhaps, shocking looks as the stars make their way up the celebrated stairs at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Below, see a list of 2026 Met Gala attendees. This story will be updated with photos as guests arrive.
Met Gala 2026 Guest List

title: Rihanna (Again) Steals the Show at 2026 Met Gala
summary: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky
story: Rihanna arrived fashionably late to the Met Gala on the arm of longtime love A$AP Rocky.

Rihanna donned a sculptural gold and silver metallic look from Maison Margiela FW25 Couture, featuring a long-sleeve top embellished in crystals and beads. She was wrapped in a ribbony gold shawl that reached down to the floor and resembled a golden cocoon.
Follow for live updates
She completed her look with metallic curls woven into her hair and large rings and other pieces of eye-catching jewelry from Briony Raymond and Dyne jewelry.
A$AP Rocky wore a pink Chanel tuxedo with black lapels.
The couple have made the annual event a kind of family affair over the years.
Rihanna stole the show on the red carpet of the 2025 Met Gala by revealing she was expecting her third child, Rocki Irish, who was born in September 2025.
Actually, it was A$AP Rocky who broke the pregnancy news just ahead of the "Diamonds" singer's entrance.
“Rihanna’s outfit tonight is ... I don’t know, whatever, just don’t cover her baby hump, you dig?” he said in an interview on the carpet.
Rihanna strolled into the gala with a look that was picture perfect for the night's theme, “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style.”
She wore a cropped jacket with a crossover necktie and a gray corseted top that did cover her baby bump.
Her floor-length skirt resembled a suit jacket, and she topped it all off with a wide-brim black hat.
As she made her way to speak with TODAY’s Al Roker, a fan jokingly called out to ask Rihanna if she’d brought her “umbrella-ella-ella, eh” — a play on her hit 2007 single “Umbrella.”
“I brought the kid!” Rihanna responded.
Rihanna has earned raves for her Met Gala looks over the years.

The singer and Fenty mogul has been a steady presence at the event since making her gala debut in 2007. She wore a white Georges Chakra dress that she paired with black fishnet gloves for her first outing.
In 2009, she wore a Dolce & Gabba tux with puffed sleeves. She topped off the look with slicked back hair.

In 2011, she put her own spin on that year's “Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty” theme by wearing a curve-hugging lace Stella McCartney gown paired with long red hair done up in a braid over her shoulder.
For 2015's “China: Through the Looking Glass” theme, she wore a regal Guo Pei-designed yellow coat-dress with long train and crown.
In 2017, she wore a wild sculptural floral Commes des Garçons gown for the gala's “Rei Kawakubo: Art Of The In-Between” theme.

In 2018, she took fashion cues from the Vatican for the gala's “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination” theme. She wore a rhinestone-encrusted minidress with a coat — and, yes, a papal headdress — designed by John Galliano for Maison Margiela.
In 2021, Rihanna wore a Balenciaga overcoat that she paired with  267 carats of Bulgari jewelry.
In 2023, she arrived an hour and a half late to pay homage to Karl Lagerfeld by wearing a custom white hooded Valentino Haute Couture gown adorned with camellia flowers and a long train.
The singer later removed the outer part of her ensemble to reveal a strappy white gown and a baby bump, after revealing her pregnancy during her Super Bowl halftime show.

title: Heidi Klum Went Just as Hard at the Met Gala as She Does for Halloween
summary: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala
story: Heidi Klum brought her love of Halloween costumes to the 2026 Met Gala.
Klum, who throws one of Hollywood’s most famous Halloween parties every year, got into the spooky spirit to honor the event’s “Fashion is Art” dress code this year.
Follow for live updates

The 52-year-old supermodel and “Project Runway” host arrived at the gala dressed as a marble statue in a custom look created by her longtime Halloween collaborator, Oscar-nominated make-up artist Mike Marino, Klum revealed on Instagram.
The look was inspired by classical sculptures such as “Veiled Vestal” by Raffaele Monti.
Marino “transformed fabric into sculpture, manipulating latex and spandex with extraordinary precision to mirror the stillness, delicacy, and illusion of carved marble,” Klum explained.
Klum appeared to be carved out of marble and then draped with a gauzy white sheet. She wore ancient-looking sandals on her feet, a simple wreath on her head and creepy looking contact lenses.
Klum also expressed her enthusiasm for this year’s gala dress code.
“I love fashion, I love art, and I especially love when the two collide,” she wrote.

Klum’s Met Gala look isn’t too far a cry from her 2025 Halloween costume. The model transformed into Medusa, the terrifying figure from Greek mythology who has snakes instead of hair. So frightening looking was Medusa that anyone who dared look at her turned to stone.
For the costume, Klum wore a prosthetic forked tongue and had fake snakes moving around on her head. Scales covered her face and body and she wore bright green contact lenses.
Klum’s husband Tom Kaulitz walked the red carpet with her dressed as one of Medusa’s victims: a stone figure.
Klum told People magazine in 2025 that she sees creating her costumes each year as a kind of “art.”

“I never take the easy way out,” she said. “People say, ‘Why the heck are you doing this?’ I do it for the art of it, of transformation, of surprising people also. It’s like a life performance art thing in a way because you see so much AI and things are being photographed, repatched, this, that and the other.”
“I always try to come up with something that is giving people an ‘ooh’ and ‘ahh’ and a wow because I want to inspire people to also do something creative,” she shares.

title: Kim Kardashian’s Met Gala 2026 Look Could Be From the Future (But It’s From the Past)
summary: Kim Kardashian appeared to have stepped out of the future when she arrived at the 2026 Met Gala
story: Kim Kardashian appeared to have stepped out of the future when she arrived at the 2026 Met Gala.
The reality star and Skims co-founder, 45, wore a bronze breastplate and an ankle-length open skirt in a daring look she collaborated on with artist Alan Jones.
Follow for live updates

“We used his body plates that he started making in the ’60s, and this is actually a mold from a model from the ‘60s that we made together, and he painted on top of it," Kardashian told E! News' La La Anthony of the pair's collaboration.
Kardashian completed her look, which nods to the event's "Fashion is Art" theme this year, with tousled bond hair and tan heels.
Kardashian made her gala debut alongside then partner Kanye West in 2013 when she was pregnant with the couple's first child, daughter North West.
Kardashian wore a custom floral Givenchy gown by designer Riccardo Tisci that showed off her baby bump.

The following year, the former couple showed up in classic Old Hollywood looks. Kardashian wore a blue strapless Lanvin gown with her hair straight and parted to the side. West wore a black tuxedo with coattails.

Fast forward to Kardashian's edgier look in 2018 when she paid tribute to the night's theme, “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination," by wearing a gold chainmail Versace dress embellished with crosses.
The following year she wore a custom corseted gown by Thierry Mugler, marking the designer's first new look in 20 years.
Kardashian made headlines again in 2021 when she showed up to the Met Gala in a dramatic black Balenciaga look that covered her from head to toe.

When Kardashian attended the 2022 gala with then-boyfriend Pete Davidson, she wore the iconic dress Marilyn Monroe wore in 1962 when she sang happy birthday to President John F. Kennedy at Madison Square Garden.
Last year, Kardashian paid homage to the gala's “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style” theme by wearing a two-piece leather Chrome Hearts look that she topped off with a leather hat.

title: Bad Bunny Ages 50 Years for 2026 Met Gala
summary: Bad Bunny once again delivered a fresh and unique look for the Met Gala.
story: Bad Bunny gave fans a glimpse of his future self at the 2026 Met Gala.

The fashion-loving rapper showed up to the star-studded event looking many years older. He wore prosthetics to add wrinkles, saggy skin and sun spots to his face and hands while his hair and beard were white.
Follow for live updates
The 32-year-old musician, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, cut a sophisticated figure wearing a black tuxedo with a large bow he designed in collaboration with Zara, reported Vogue.

"Part of my life (is) always try to do something different and and this day of the Met is a perfect day to you explore and be creative and express yourself in a different way. So that’s, that’s what I’m doing tonight," Bad Bunny told E! News' La La Anthony on the event's red carpet.
When Anthony asked Bad Bunny how many hours it took to create his look, he cheekily responded, "53 years, exactly, 53 years."
"So it took a little bit, but it worth it. I hope he looked good. Do I look good?" he asked.
Bad Bunny's bold Met Gala look is hardly his first. In the past, he's shown up to the event in gender-bending ensembles that frequently pay tribute to his Puerto Rico homeland.
For his Met Gala debut in 2022, Bad Bunny gave the night's gilded glamour dress code a Puerto Rican twist.
“I researched what was going on during the Gilded Age in my country, in Puerto Rico,” he told Vogue ahead of the event.

Working with Burberry’s Riccardo Tisci, the pair “mixed the men’s looks with women’s” from that time to come up with a custom ensemble featuring a tan puff-sleeve trench coat and skirt over classic blue collared shirt and black tie.
Bad Bunny topped off the look by wearing his hair in an updo and using a pair of vintage hand-held eyeglasses.

In 2023, the multi-Grammy winner dazzled in an elegant and angelic white suit with a backless blazer and 26 foot flower train to celebrate the Met's  new exhibition “Karl Lagerfeld: A Line of Beauty.”

He returned to co-chair the Met Gala in 2024 in another incredible ensemble. For that year's celebration, he wore a custom look from the Maison Margiela Artisanal Collection by John Galliano featuring a black satin corset under a navy barathea wool smoking jacket, Vogue reported.
He completed the look with a reverse-swatched navy hat, dark gloves and a dark floral fabric bouquet resembling Flor de Maga, the national flower of Puerto Rico.
In 2025, Bad Bunny wore a chocolate brown Prada suit that he paired with gold crystal-adorned gloves, a woven pava hat made from palm leaves that celebrated his Puerto Rican heritage.
He also donned a diamond brooch in the shape of the Flor de Maga.

title: Beyoncé Attends Met Gala For the First Time in a Decade Alongside Daughter Blue Ivy
summary: Beyoncé is making her grand return to the Met Gala for the first time in a decade
story: Beyoncé is making her grand return to the Met Gala for the first time in a decade.
The singer, 44, who last walked the event's red carpet in 2016, is one of the gala's co-chairs this year alongside Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams and Anna Wintour.
Follow for live updates
Beyoncé's stunning 2026 look reflects the year's dress code: "Fashion As Art," inspired by the Costume Institute's spring 2026 exhibit, “Costume Art."
She arrived on the carpet wearing an embellished ensemble by Olivier Rousteing. Alongside her was her oldest daughter, Blue Ivy Carter, 14, who made her Met Gala debut with her mom.

“I think it’s really just experiencing this through the eyes of blue and being able to relax,” Beyoncé told La La Anthony for E! News on the carpet. “And I think for me, I’m wearing Olivier Rousteing, who is someone that’s been so loyal to me, and I’ve done so many incredible, iconic looks with him. So it’s really about representing him.”
Beyoncé's Met Gala looks have evolved over the years.
She played it safe when she made her debut at the gala in 2008 wearing a strapless pink Armani Privé gown.
She made her next appearance at the gala in 2011 in a black hip-hugging Emilio Pucci gown that featured eye-catching gold embroidery. The gown featured a cut-out at the bust and a flared tulle skirt.
In 2012, she earned raves for her striking black and purple Givenchy gown. But that headline-making look almost never happened.

The then new mom to daughter Blue Ivy, who was born in January 2012, had planned to skip the gala to continue rehearsing for her "The Mrs. Carter Show World Tour," Ty Hunter, the singer's longtime stylist, told WWD in 2017.
Hours before the gala, the singer changed her mind.
“She was, like, ‘The Met is today.’ I was, like, ‘Yeah.’ And she was, like, ‘I think I want to go,’” Hunter recalled.

“Literally within a day or a couple of hours all of that happened — and it ended up being one of her most talked-about looks,” Hunter added. “I was, like, ‘Oh my God, you want to go to what? The Met?’”

"She had just had Blue and the world hadn’t seen her," he continued. "To come back with a wow after being pregnant for so long and just showing that you can still be sexy and hot as a parent, (that’s) one of the reasons that dress got a lot of attention.”

In 2015, the Grammy winner got playful with the event’s dress code — “China: Through the Looking Glass” — by donning a sheer Givenchy Haute Couture by Riccardo Tisci gown adorned with Swarovski crystals, rhinestones and other gems.
In 2016, Beyoncé's most recent trip to the Met Gala until now, the singer paired dark eye makeup with a skin-tight peach-colored latex gown by Givenchy.

title: Kendall Jenner Is Sheer Elegance at the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Kendall Jenner looked just like a Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala
story: Kendall Jenner looked just like a Greek goddess at the 2026 Met Gala.
The 30-year-old model tapped Zac Posen, creative director of GAP, to create a GapStudio look that honored the gala's dress code this year, "Fashion Is Art."
Follow for live updates
Jenner's stunning gown was was inspired by "Winged Victory of Samothrace," the second-century Greek statue of the goddess of victory that greets visitors to the Louvre museum in Paris.
Posen told E! News' La La Anthony he came up with the idea for the look after twisting an old Gap T-shirt to mimic the goddess's flowing tunic.
"I like that idea that art is transcendent. It’s not owned by an institution. It’s owned by the people. It’s living," said Posen.
"I like this because it’s the goddess of Nike, the goddess of game and play and of victory.
Posen added that Jenner's look may also include a "surprise" to be revealed inside the Met.
Jenner and her sisters Kylie Jenner and Kim Kardashian have been staples of the gala for years.
Kendall Jenner has been to a dozen Met Galas since she made her debut at the event as a teenager in 2014. She wore a champagne-colored strapless gown by Topshop paired with Tabitha Simmons heels.
In 2017, she won raves for wearing a sheer gown La Perla Haute Couture Collection.
In 2018, she donned an Off White jumpsuit and attended the event with the label’s founder, Virgil Abloh.
“We worked together really heavily on creating this look for me, most of our conversations were over FaceTime,” Jenner recalled while revisiting the outfit for Vogue’s “Life in Looks.”
“You always felt comfortable and safe in his arms," she added of Abloh, who died in 2021.
The following year, she and sister Kylie Jenner arrived together wearing feathery Versace ensembles, with Kendall opting for a bright orange number.
In 2021, Kendall Jenner wore a semi-sheer Givenchy gown full of embellishments that was inspired by Audrey Hepburn’s character in "My Fair Lady."
She pulled out all the stops in for the 2024 gala by walking the red carpet in an archival Givenchy dress from Alexander McQueen’s fall 1999 couture collection.
“I made a dream list of who I would ideally want to go with, and I would have died to wear something by Alexander McQueen,” Jenner told Vogue.

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Stars Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams Go Shirtless and Sleeveless for Met Gala Debuts
summary: The breakout stars of "Heated Rivalry," Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams, have made their Met Gala debuts in 2026
story: The breakout stars of "Heated Rivalry," Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams, have made their Met Gala debuts in 2026.

The fashion-forward actors, who play pro hockey rivals-turned-lovers Ilya Rozanov and Shane Hollander on the HBO series, walked the carpet at the annual event, which raises funds for the Metropolitan Museum of Art's Costume Institute.
Follow live updates.
Storrie was first on the Met Gala red carpet, wearing a custom Saint Laurent look.
Shortly before his debut on the carpet, Storrie was photographed leaving The Carlyle Hotel nearby on his way to the Met.
Williams showed up shortly after in a custom Balenciaga look designed by Pierpaolo Piccioli.
Storrie and Williams have made headlines for their fashionable looks since launching a whirlwind press tour after "Heated Rivalry" premiered on HBO in November 2025.
Williams made his runway debut in January at Milan Men’s Fashion Week when he opened the Dsquared2 show, reported Vogue.
He and Storrie camped it up when they presented together at the 2026 Golden Globes.

Storrie wore a custom tailored Saint Laurent suit that he paired with Tiffany & Co. jewelry while Williams wore a custom Giorgio Armani white dinner jacket with black pants and an unbuttoned white silk shirt, reported Fashion magazine.
During Paris Fashion Week in March, Storrie attended an Yves Saint Laurent fashion show to see the brand's Fall Winter 2026 collection, according to the publication.
Storrie wore a light brown suit in Saint Laurent’s signature cut. He tucked his silky brown tie into a yellow shirt — a look right out of brand’s Spring 2026 menswear collection, Vogue reported.

title: Sabrina Carpenter Explains Her Met Gala 2026 Look Inspired by ‘Sabrina’ the Movie
summary: What did Sabrina Carpenter wear to the 2026 Met Gala? See how she interpretend the Fashion Is Art theme and dress code.
story: Sabrina Carpenter brought old-school glamour to the "Costume Art" theme of the 2026 Met Gala.
The "Manchild" singer, 26, paid tribute to this year's dress code, "Fashion is Art," by wearing a custom Dior tulle dress by Jonathan Anderson.
Follow for live updates

Carpenter's look not only emulated Hollywood legend Audrey Hepburn's style in the 1954 movie "Sabrina," it included rhinestone images that resembled film strips featuring Hepburn and her co-stars in the romance, Humphrey Bogart and William Holden.

“Sabrina Carpenter in literal film fashion collapses directly into medium. The material isn’t just decorative, it IS the concept, turning the body into a moving surface for image and narrative," celebrity stylist Julie Matos tells TODAY.com.

“It’s all made of film, which is my dream,” Carpenter told La La Anthony in her red carpet interview. “Jonathan Anderson, the genius that he is, and Dior, the whole team; I can’t even express my love and gratitude for them. We just finished all of my Coachella looks together, and then they hopped right into making this dream dress. And Jared Ellner, my stylist, we put it all together, and it’s amazing. It’s just from the movie “Sabrina,” which is one of my favorite films of all time.”
Carpenter also this year sits on the gala's host committee alongside Teyana Taylor, Doja Cat, Misty Copeland, Lisa and other stars.
Carpenter leads with creativity when it comes to her Met Gala looks.
Last year, she showed up to the gala wearing a pantless burgundy Louis Vuitton suit featuring a lengthy train.

At the time, the singer said Pharrell Williams, who designed the look for Louis Vuitton, told her the inspiration was her height. “’You’re quite short, so no pants for you,’” she told La La Anthony on the Vogue live stream. “So here we are.”
The previous year, the singer attended the extravaganza wearing a coordinated look with her then-boyfriend, “Saltburn” actor Barry Keoghan.
For the “Garden of Time” theme, Carpenter donned a custom black strapless Oscar de la Renta gown featuring a mermaid bodice and voluminous baby blue satin skirt.
Keoghan wore a dark olive velvet Burberry three-piece suit. He completed his look with a top hat and black oxford boots.
Carpenter made her Met Gala debut in 2022 wearing a gold Paco Rabanne gown with a sweeping train, reported British Vogue.
Carpenter told the publication she was thrilled to be a part of fashion’s biggest night.
“To me, it’s the Super Bowl,” she said. “I love seeing so many incredible people come together under one roof for art and fashion.”

title: Kylie Jenner Bleaches Her Brows For 2026 Met Gala Look
summary: Kylie Jenner stepped into the spotlight at the 2026 Met Gala in a sleeveless Schiaparelli gown
story: Kylie Jenner stepped into the spotlight at the 2026 Met Gala in a sleeveless Schiaparelli gown.
The youngest Kardashian-Jenner sister's gown featured a tan corset top and cream-colored skirt.
Follow for live updates
She paired the look with heaps of eye-catching diamond jewelry and also appeared to bleach her eyebrows blond for the event, joking in her Instagram Story, "I don’t have any brows, wish me luck."
Jenner, 28, who showed up without longtime love Timothée Chalamet, has been a staple at the Met Gala since making her debut at the event in 2016.

That year, the Kylie Cosmetics founder teamed up with her mom, Kris Jenner, and older sister Kim Kardashian to wear designs by Olivier Rousteing for Balmain in honor of the night's "Manus X Machina: Fashion in an Age of Technology" theme.
In 2018, the new mom at the time — daughter Stormi was born in February 2018 — walked the gala's red carpet in a black Alexander Wang gown with then-partner Travis Scott.
She turned heads in 2019 when she wore a sheer lilac Versace gown that was trimmed with painted ostrich feathers with a nod to the night's "Camp: Notes on Fashion" theme.
In 2023, she wore an asymmetrical red gown designed by Haider Ackermann for Jean Paul Gaultier.
She followed up a year later by wearing a stunning cream-colored sculptural dress by Oscar de la Renta.

In 2025, she walked the gala's carpet in a curve-hugging  Ferragamo gown featuring a low-waisted skirt and thigh-high slit. The look was helmed by designer Maximilian Davis, who doubled as Jenner's date for the evening.

title: Katy Perry Draws Eyes at 2026 Met Gala With Unique Mask
summary: Katy Perry made a surprise visit to the 2026 Met Gala that included a dramatic face reveal
story: Katy Perry made a surprise visit to the 2026 Met Gala that included a dramatic face reveal.
The "Roar' singer arrived to the event and posed immediately with "Heated Rivalry" star Connor Storrie.
Follow for live updates

At first, Perry remained anonymous in a mesh and silver mask that looked like something out of a futuristic fencing event. She paired her intriguing mask with a white off-the-shoulder Stella McCartney gown and long white opera gloves.
As she made her way across the event's garden-inspired carpet, Perry removed the silver portion of her mask to reveal her identity.
Perry has a history of bringing bold — some might say bizarre —  looks to the Met Gala.
For the event's “Manus x Machina: Fashion in an Age of Technology” theme in 2016, the space-loving singer bleached her eyebrows blond and wore blue mascara to complement her glossy black updo.
In 2019, she walked the gala's carpet dressed as a giant chandelier. Perry's kooky outfit, which was embellished with thousands of Swarovski crystals, included a working light fixture.
“There is a built-in corset consisting of 18 steel bones and two hidden battery packs to illuminate the lights,” Perry's longtime collaborator Moschino designer Jeremy Scott told Vogue at the time.
“I mean who wants to wear a chandelier that doesn’t light up!”
After the 2019 gala, Perry famously showed up to an after party dressed as a cheeseburger.
Perry last attended the gala in 2022 wearing a high-low Oscar de la Renta dress.

“I feel great,” she told Vogue during a red carpet interview. “I mean it’s not my first rodeo, so I feel like I can relax. You know, last time I came to the Met, I was a cheeseburger. I loved it, and I was like, ‘What am I going to do tonight?’

title: Zoë Kravitz Hides Her Left Hand On the Met Gala Red Carpet
summary: Zoë Kravitz is returning to the Met Gala looking like a beautiful goth bride
story: Zoë Kravitz is returning to the Met Gala looking like a beautiful goth bride.
The "Big Little Lies" star, 37, walked the garden-inspired carpet of the 2026 Met Gala wearing a lacy black Saint Laurent gown designed by Anthony Vaccarello.
Follow for live updates
The gown featured a basque waist — a trendy look in wedding dresses.

Kravits also wore several eye-catching pieces of jewelry but coyly kept the new diamond ring on her left finger hidden. The ring has sparked engagement rumors about her and her pop star boyfriend Harry Styles since Kravitz debuted it in April.

(People magazine reported she and Styles, who've been dating since August 2025, are indeed engaged set to tie the knot. TODAY.com did not hear back from Kravitz's or Styles' representatives when contacted for comment.)
Kravitz made her Met Gala debut in 2008 when she was 18. She wore a a loose mint green velvet dress and fur stole that would have looked at home in the Roaring 1920s.
Over the years, her Met Gala looks have gotten a lot edgier.
In 2015, she wore a slinky silver chainmail dress designed by Alexander Wang.
For 2016's “Manus x Machina: Fashion in an Age of Technology” theme, Kravitz wore a lacy Valentino mini-dress paired with a  face mask and tulle-covered heels.
In 2017, she arrived with a short platinum blond pixie cut and wore a pale pink Oscar de la Renta gown adorned with a cape.
The following year, she bared a lot of skin in a one-armed, lace Saint Laurent dress held up by just two bows on the side.
In 2021, the last time Kravitz attended the Met Gala, she got even more daring when she showed up in a see-through Saint Laurent gown.

title: Venus Williams Sparkles With All Crystals at the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Venus Williams, who attended the Met Gala with husband Andrea Preti, didn't have to look far to create a look that honored the event's theme, “Costume Art,” and its dress code, “Fashion is Art.”
story: Venus Williams is serving in a Swarovski at the 2026 Met Gala.

The 45-year-old tennis legend, who attended the event with husband Andrea Preti, didn't have to look far to create a look that honored the event's theme, “Costume Art,” and its dress code, “Fashion is Art.”
Follow live updates
Williams wore a stunning Swarovski crystal mesh gown inspired by Robert Pruitt’s portrait of her, "Venus Williams, Double Portrait," commissioned by the National Portrait Gallery.
Williams' necklace was a re-creation of the Wimbledon-plate-inspired necklace she wears in the portrait, which also honors Black tennis stars who came before her.

“It reflects not just my journey, but the legacy of those who came before me — especially pioneers like Althea Gibson and Arthur Ashe, who paved the way," Williams said of the portrait when she spoke to Vogue ahead of the Met Gala.
"It felt like a personal way to connect with ‘Costume Art,’ using fashion to tell a story about legacy, progress, and honoring those who made it possible," she added.
Petri stood by Williams side looking dapper in a dark tuxedo.
Williams is one of the co-chairs of the gala this year along with Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman and Anna Wintour.

The seven-time Grand Slam singles champion made headlines for bringing “tenniscore” couture to the 2025 Met Gala, which had the theme  “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style” and a dress code of “Tailored For You.”
Williams walked the red carpet wearing a forest-green Lacoste mini-dress with a bedazzled polo-style collar. She completed her look with a floor-length green coat with fringe lapel and sleeves.
Williams has attended multiple Met Galas, dating back to her 2004 debut.
“I think I was the first athlete to come,” she noted in 2024 at the Met Gala to E! News. “And then I took a long break and then I came back again like five years ago.”
At the 2024 gala, Williams wore a shimmering Marc Jacobs mirrored gown  — and accidentally broke one of the mirrors before hitting the red carpet, she told E! News.
“It’s based off the theme of today, we’re showing all these dresses that are too fragile to wear,” Williams said of her look's alignment with the 2024 dress code of “Garden of Time."
“So he wanted to bring in the fragility of mirrors,” she added before quipping, “I only broke one.”
The same year, Williams, who is married to former model and actor Andrea Preti, told Harper’s Bazaar she believed tennis and fashion go hand in hand.
“Tennis never goes out of style. It’s always trending!” said the athlete. “I’ve always loved seeing people embracing tennis-core and incorporating it into everyday style in their own unique way.”

title: Nicole Kidman Arrives with Daughter Sunday Rose, 17, As Her Date to the 2026 Met Gala
summary: Nicole Kidman is a Met Gala regular. For the 2026 event, she is a co-chair and dressed to impress. See her look.
story: Nicole Kidman arrived to the 2026 Met Gala red carpet with a special date — her fashion model daughter Sunday Rose, 17.

The newly single "Scarpetta" star, 58, who split from ex-husband Keith Urban in 2025, wore a red sequined Chanel gown with long sleeves and feather details. Her long blond hair was worn straight and parted down the middle.
Follow live updates
Sunday Rose wore a strapless pink gown with billowy floral embellishments that matched her earrings.
The stylish duo's look paid homage to the gala’s theme of “Costume Art” and its dress code: “Fashion is Art."

Kidman is one of the co-chairs of this year's Met Gala alongside Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Anna Wintour. The night marks Kidman's third time as the event's co-chair, having previously served in 2003 and 2005.
Over the years, the “Moulin Rouge” star has made headlines for her gala looks since her debut at the event in 2003.

That year, Kidman nodded to the night's theme — “Goddess: The Classical Mode” — by wearing a sheer one-shouldered gown with crystal embellishments designed by Tom Ford for Gucci. She paired the look with long fringe diamond earrings.
In 2005, she wore a strapless navy Chanel gown that featured intricate black beading.

In 2016, the Oscar winner honored the event’s edgy “Manus X Machina: Fashion in an Age of Technology” theme by wearing a heavily embellished black Alexander McQueen gown with a plunging neckline and cutaways in the torso.
She topped off her look with a cape adorned with beaded moons and stars and a delicate crown.

In 2023, Kidman got high marks for repurposing a pink tulle Chanel gown with ostrich feathers that she originally wore in a 2003 Chanel No. 5 commercial helmed by filmmaker Baz Luhrmann, who directed her in “Moulin Rouge,” “Australia” and other movies.
In 2025, Oscar winner won rave reviews again for wearing a re-creation of a 1950s black Balenciaga gown.

title: What Is the 2026 Met Gala Theme? All About 'Fashion Is Art'
summary: The 2026 Met Gala is today, which means designers, stylists and their A-list celebrity clients are putting the final touches on their looks for fashion’s biggest night
story: The 2026 Met Gala is today, which means designers, stylists and their A-list celebrity clients are putting the final touches on their looks for fashion’s biggest night.
The gala, which is held annually to raise funds for the Met’s Costume Institute, is in conversation with the museum’s spring exhibition. This year, the exhibition is “Costume Art.”

The exhibit takes garments and works of art from the Met’s vast collection, juxtaposing them to create pairings that, “illuminate the indivisible connection between clothing and the body” and as well as the “complex interplay between artistic representations of the body and fashion as an embodied art form,” says the museum.
This year’s Met Gala is co-chaired by a team of A-list celebs: Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams and Anna Wintour.

Zoë Kravitz, Sabrina Carpenter, Teyana Taylor, Doja Cat, Gwendoline Christie, Lena Dunham and Misty Copeland, Elizabeth Debicki, Alex Consani, Paloma Elsesser, LISA, Chloe Malle, Sam Smith, Lauren Wasser, Anna Weyant, A’ja Wilson and Yseult also join as members of the 2026 Gala Host Committee.
The Dress Code: ‘Fashion Is Art’
“Fashion Is Art” is the dress code for this year’s star-studded event, held May 4 at the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
Gala gawkers can expect to see the bodies treated as works of art and garments paying homage to famous paintings and sculptures, Julie Matos, a New York-based celebrity stylist tells TODAY.com.

“The theme is so important. I don’t think enough people see fashion as art, so it’s nice to bring that concept to the forefront especially as the Met Gala has become more mainstream,” says Matos, whose clients have included Gwyneth Paltrow, Demi Moore, Serena Williams and Carrie Underwood.
The Expectations

With a “Fashion is Art” theme, Matos predicts eye-catching gowns that lift imagery from great works of art, as well garments that look as though they themselves could be “hanging on the walls of The Met.”

Matos says inspiration can be found in designers Viktor Horsting and Rolf Snoere’s 2015 “Wearable Art” fashion show. The show’s models, reported WWD, wore gowns that looked like paintings and sculptures, with some literally wearing wood canvas frames as part of their ensembles.
Fashion lovers should expect to see similar “sculptural construction pieces” that stretch the boundaries of what clothes can be at this year’s gala, says Matos.
She also foresees garments created out of materials artists might work with, like the strapless Balmain gown singer Tyla wore at her 2024 Met Gala debut.
The gown was created out of sand and micro-crystal studs to represent the night’s theme, “The Sand of Time,” Vogue reported at the time.
“It was sculptural and gorgeous,” recalls Matos.
Maggie Gillette, a fashion and lifestyle writer who co-founded Chicago Fashion Week, expects to see “a lot of avant-garde and surrealist fashion.”
“This is definitely a time to push fashion boundaries,” she says.
Matos is counting on daring looks from some of her favorite Met Gala celebrities, including Rihanna and A$AP Rocky.
“I am a huge, huge fan of Rihanna. I feel like she can do no wrong when it comes to fashion and the Met Gala,” she says.
“I am excited to see Rosalía too, especially because this year’s theme really encapsulates her idea of music as art and fashion,” she continues.
Celebrities from the music world often make headlines for boasting the Gala’s most creative looks. “They just go for it. They understand the assignment,” Matos explains.
Although the guest list is only revealed the evening of the event, other celebs Matos is excited to see each year include Bad Bunny, Harry Styles, Teyana Taylor, Pharrell and Lady Gaga.
“All of them commit to the theme, you know? They don’t just dress for the red carpet,” she says. “They really want to perform it and they’re intentional and fearless.”

title: Met Gala 2026 Livestream: When It Starts and How to Watch
summary: The 2026 Met Gala is here. Find out what time and how to watch and stream the red carpet arrivals.
story: May has arrived, which means the 2026 Met Gala, the biggest fashion event of the year, is here.

On May 4, stars who scored a coveted invite will pose on the famous steps of the Metropolitan Museum of Art dressed in designer outfits that reflect the theme: “Costume Art.” Beyoncé, Venus Williams and Nicole Kidman will be leading by example, following the “Fashion Is Art” dress code as this year’s co-chairs, along with Anna Wintour.
Follow live coverage

The Beyhive is highly anticipating the music icon’s appearance as she hasn’t attended the extravagant event since 2016. Other stars who are expected to walk the Met’s grand steps are host committee co-chairs Anthony Vaccarello and Zoë Kravitz, as well as fellow members Sabrina Carpenter, Teyana Taylor, Lena Dunham, Misty Copeland, A’ja Wilson, Doja Cat, Sam Smith, Agela Bassett, Chase Sui Wonders and more. Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez Bezos are honorary chairs.
TODAY’s Sheinelle Jones will be on the red carpet to chat with the celebs as they enter the museum.
So, how will all the guests interpret the theme and execute the dress code? Well, according to Vogue, the event will highlight the “centrality of the dressed body.”

That broad description will surely lead to elaborate outfits and a few dramatic reveals. Want to make sure you don’t miss any of the must-see arrivals? Find out how to watch the 2026 Met Gala red carpet livestream, below.
When Is the 2026 Met Gala?
Every year, the Met Gala, dubbed the biggest night in fashion, is held on the first Monday in May. The 2026 Met Gala is taking place on May 4.
What Time Does the 2026 Met Gala Start?
The first group of stars will begin to strut up the Metropolitan Museum of Art steps when the red carpet opens at 3 p.m. PT/ 6 p.m. ET.
Where Does the 2026 Met Gala Take Place?

Every year, the Met Gala is held at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City and acts as a fundraiser for the museum. The proceeds from the event go to the Costume Institute, helping fund its exhibitions, publications, acquisitions and operations, according to Vogue.
This year’s exhibition, featuring the “Costume Art” theme, will be open from May 10 through Jan. 10, 2027.
How to Watch the 2026 Met Gala Arrivals
For the 2026 Met Gala, the red carpet livestream will be hosted by Vogue and broadcast live on the fashion magazine’s website and other digital platforms, including YouTube and TikTok.
Ashley Graham, La La Anthony and Cara Delevingne will work as co-hosts, and Emma Chamberlain is returning as the magazine’s red carpet correspondent.

Additionally, fashionistas can watch “Live From E!: Met Gala 2026” airing on the network starting at 3 p.m. PT/ 6 p.m. ET with Zuri Hall as the host. The special will be broadcast again on the West Coast at 8 p.m. PT.

Hall will be joined by E!’s panel of fashion experts: Justin Sylvester, Keltie Knight and Zanna Roberts Rassi, as well as “Summer House” star Ciara Miller, “The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City” cast member Bronwyn Newport and former WNBA player and current sports analyst Renee Montgomery. Plus, designer Christian Siriano and celebrity hairstylist Chris Appleton will be sharing their thoughts on every look, too.
Fans can sign up for DirecTV, Hulu + Live TV or Peacock to stream all the action. They can also visit E! Online, the E! News app and any of E! social media pages.

TV personality Erin Lim Rhodes will be on hand to see the celebrities who stop by The Pierre Hotel before making their way to the museum. She will host E!’s livestream online that begins around 2:30 p.m. PT/ 5:30 p.m. ET.

After the event, E!’s Will Marfuggi, internet personality Robyn DelMonte and “RuPaul’s Drag Race” alum Monét X Change will break down all the major moments on the “RE!CAP: Met Gala 2026” after show starting at 6:30 p.m. PT/ 9:30 p.m. ET. Tune in via E!’s social media pages, website or news app.
Who Is Attending the 2026 Met Gala?
While the Met Gala does not reveal an official guest list ahead of time, the following co-chair and committee members are expected to attend:

title: Nikki Glaser Shares How She Prepared for Breakout Moment at Tom Brady Roast
summary: Nikki Glaser was already a star in the comedy world when she reached a whole new audience by doing what she does best: mercilessly roasting celebrities.
story: Nikki Glaser was already a star in the comedy world when she reached a whole new audience by doing what she does best: mercilessly roasting celebrities.

On a stage filled with some of the world's biggest comics, Glaser was a breakout star of the 2024 Netflix special, "The Roast of Tom Brady." Her hilarious zingers at the expense of the legendary NFL quarterback and current Fox Sports broadcaster led to her hosting the Golden Globes, a gig she's since secured for a third year in 2027.

Glaser, 41, spoke with Willie Geist on TODAY's Sunday Sitdown on May 3 about how she prepared for her breakthrough moment at the Brady roast, which came after decades on the standup comedy circuit and memorable appearances on Comedy Central roasts of other celebrities.

"It’s weird, because I don’t know these people," she said. "I don’t care about football, and I don’t really know why (Brady) is so great. So I just had to do my research and read his book and watch all of his interviews, and just get to a place where I was like, 'Oh my God, Tom Brady.' Like, where I believed in it, and I understood how great he was, so that I can then dismantle him."
She scorched Brady on everything from his divorce from Gisele Bündchen to his cryptocurrency investments gone wrong.
“Tom Brady, five-time Super Bowl MVP, most career wins, most career touchdowns, you have seven rings. Well, eight now that Gisele gave hers back, but..." she said in one line.

Glaser, whose new comedy special "Good Girl" is streaming on Hulu, also shared her reaction to people acting like she had been "discovered" after the Brady roast, even though she had worked as a comedian for years.
"No, I don’t mind it," she said. "I kind of like sometimes that people think that I’m just naturally this good, and that it hasn’t been 22 years leading up to this moment."
The comedian's wheelhouse is roasts, where she has delivered one stinging line after another.

"I really think the safest place for free speech that we have is a roast, because the things that get said, you would be canceled for saying them anywhere else," she said. "It’s your job to go there."

Glaser is known for finding that sweet spot of going just far enough for crowds to laugh and wince at the same time. In "Good Girl," she hit topics like aging, fame and her sex life for a crowd in her hometown of St. Louis that included her parents, E.J. and Julie, and she didn't hold back.

"Usually when they come to my live shows I will literally say into the microphone, 'Hey Dad, you might want to go out to the merch booth and get a T-shirt right now for the next 15 minutes, I think you’d be safe,'" she said.
"But for the special I couldn’t really tell them to leave because we were at a taping, and I just had to disassociate, and they do, too," she added.

"I’ve become immune over the years," E.J. Glaser told Willie on TODAY. "But when she first started doing this, we’d go to clubs with our friends. On the way out my friends would be like, 'Sorry, dude.'"
E.J. and Julie Glaser have supported their daughter's comedy dreams since she was a struggling freshman at the University of Colorado.
"We believed in her because it was an escape," Julie Glaser said.

"There was nothing I was exceptional at, and I was just dying to be seen," Nikki Glaser said. "I was kind of in the throes of a crazy eating disorder. I was just kind of wasting away, and so I just turned up my personality. I just became a lot funnier than I had ever been before just so I could make friends and make people forget about what they were looking at. And that was the first time I started hearing like, 'You should be a comedian.'"
Glaser studied legendary comic Sarah Silverman before trying standup herself at a comedy showcase on campus.

"I called my dad backstage," she said. "I was just like, 'I know what I want to do. I’m going to be a comedian.' And it was like, 'OK, well, let’s figure out this eating disorder, because this is going to stand in my way.' It just gave me something to live for."
Glaser is known as a fearless comic, but she admittedly was a little intimidated when she first hosted the Golden Globes in 2025.

"Oh, that was terrifying," she said. "I think it was (former Golden Globes host) Ricky (Gervais) who told me like, 'You aren’t one of them. You’re not an A-lister. And no offense, but we’re comedians. We’re clowns. And they want to feel like you are just so in awe of them.'"
Glaser's performance was so well received that she is now in line to host the awards for the third straight time.
"The second year I could walk out there, and I sensed that their faces weren’t as pained," she said. "There was a little bit more leaning in of like, 'I hope she might talk about me.'"
While lobbing one-liners at a group of Hollywood's biggest stars is no sweat for Glaser, there is one person whom she blushed at the thought of being able to work with one day.

"If I’m putting one out there, I would like to be in a Taylor Swift music video someday," she said. "I’m just the biggest Swiftie, and I just love the way she directs. I would never approach her with it or anything, and I would be embarrassed if she ever saw this clip.

"She’s aware of me, and so sweet. But it’s too overwhelming. I honestly am blushing. This is the only thing where I will get a little bit cagey, because I just want to be respectful," Glaser added. "She doesn’t even have to be my friend. I’m not asking for much, and I’ll do my best, and she can cut me out of it. I just want to watch her work."

title: Are Zoë Kravitz and Harry Styles Engaged? Jewelry Experts Analyze Her Ring
summary: Are Zoë Kravitz and Harry Styles taking their relationship to the next level?
story: Are Zoë Kravitz and Harry Styles taking their relationship to the next level? The couple are reportedly engaged, according to People magazine, whose sources say they have started sharing the news with a “small circle” and she’s been showing her ring to friends.

Per People, the couple have been linked since August 2025. They’ve been photographed together throughout the last eight months; in the most recent photos, she’s sporting a stunning sparkler on her left ring finger.
The couple has not commented publicly on the engagement reports nor on Kravitz’s ring, and NBC News has reached out to Styles’ and Kravitz’s reps for comment on People’s story.
Meantime, TODAY.com spoke with jewelry experts who took a closer look at Kravitz’s ring. Here’s what they had to say.
The Ring Is Likely 7 to 10 Carats
La Joya Jewelry founder Nishit Mehta was “gobsmacked” at the size of the stone when he first saw a photo of Kravitz’s ring.
The third-generation diamond expert says the ring is likely in the 7 to 10-carat range.

When it comes to the monetary value of the ring, the cost depends on a number of factors, according to Jaclyn Elbaum, the co-founder of Audry Rose, a jewelry brand that Taylor Swift has been spotted wearing.
“I would estimate $500,000+. The exact value would depend heavily on the diamond’s color, clarity and origin, but it’s undoubtedly a significant stone,” she says.
The Ring Appears to Be a Cushion or Mine Cut
The jewelry pros we spoke to believe the ring is a cushion or mine cut but say it’s hard to be sure without seeing it up close.
“The ring appears to feature a spectacular antique cushion or possibly an old mine cut diamond,” Mehta says.
Elbaum is leaning toward a cushion cut.

“From what I can see, the center stone appears to be an elongated cushion cut diamond, which is having a major moment right now,” she says. “It has that beautiful balance of softness and structure. It’s more romantic than an emerald cut, but more directional than a classic cushion.”
The Ring Features a Gold Bezel Setting
While looking at photos of the ring, Elbaum was immediately struck by the gold bezel setting.
“The gold gives the diamond a softness and warmth that you don’t always get with traditional prong settings. It feels intentional and slightly understated, despite the scale,” she says.
The setting looks like a “full or semi-bezel in yellow gold” and is likely 18 karat, she adds.
“The bezel not only frames the stone in a very clean, architectural way, but also makes the diamond feel more wearable and less overtly flashy,” Elbaum says.
She notes that she’s seen a “strong shift” toward bezel settings lately since many people are moving “away from more traditional prong styles.”
“That said, the execution here feels elevated enough that it won’t date itself. It’s the kind of ring that will still feel relevant decades from now,” she says.
Mehta adds that Kravitz’s ring is “incredibly safe to wear.”

“Because the diamond is protected by that smooth bezel rim of solid gold, it won’t snag on sweaters, scratch her or get knocked loose easily when she is running around the city. It is a ring designed to be lived in, not just looked at,” he says.
The Ring Is a ‘Sharp Break’ From Fragile Settings
As for the ring’s band, Mehta says it’s a “solid, rounded cigar style yellow gold band.”
“It provides a beautiful structural balance to the sheer size of the diamond,” he says.
Overall, Mehta says, the ring is “bold and unapologetically heavy.”
“It is a sharp break from the intricate, fragile settings we’ve seen dominate the last decade. It is minimalist while still being incredibly classic,” he says.
The Ring Suits Kravitz’s Style, One Expert Says
Elbaum says the ring perfectly captures Kravitz.

“Her style has always balanced classic and cool in a very effortless way, and this ring feels like a direct reflection of that,” she says. “It’s refined without being overly traditional and has just enough edge to feel modern. It doesn’t try too hard, which is very in line with her aesthetic.”

title: Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen Granted Mutual Protective Orders, Custody Remains the Same For Now
summary: Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen appeared in court on April 30 to discuss their dueling protective orders and custody of their 2-year-old son.
story: Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen have been granted mutual protective orders, a Utah district court judge decided on April 30.

“The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star and her ex-boyfriend appeared in court to discuss their dueling protective orders, stemming from a number of domestic violence charges and allegations, as well as the custody of their 2-year-old son, Ever.

Commissioner Russell Minas stated that the former couple would need to stay 100 feet stay away from each other. He explained that they are three year protective orders that will expire on their own. They are not criminal orders, but if an incident occurs, arrests can be made and charges can be filed that can be elevated to misdemeanors or third-degree felonies, Minas said.
When it comes to their custody agreement, he said he needed to “think a little bit more” and would need until May 11, the latest, to make any recommendations or modifications.
As of now, their parent time would stay the same, meaning Mortensen has custody and Paul is allowed eight hours of supervised time with Ever per week.

The judge stated that if he has any changes when it comes to the custody agreement, he will modify the protective order and they will have a review hearing to go over the changes. He did note that he is “contemplating lifting supervision.”
During the hearing, the judge touched on the importance of the two putting their issues behind them for the future of their child and co-parenting.

“You have to figure out how to function as co-parents. This has been dysfunctional. This is a lifelong proposition between the two,” he said, adding, “You’re linked together for the remainder of your lives, you’re going to be the grandparents of the same grandkids someday, be at the same graduations, their same weddings.”

He stated that he wants to work with both of them to restore their relationship. However, he noted, “I think right now you need to stay away from each other” and there needs to be real consequences for their actions.
Paul and Mortensen did not speak during the hearing.

In a statement to TODAY.com, Paul’s attorney Eric M. Swinyard called the hearing “a significant step forward as the court entered protective orders requiring that Taylor’s ex-partner stays far away from her.”

“Taylor was incredibly candid with the court that she is not perfect and owned her faults, which is in direct contrast to how the other party presented their argument, despite evidence and input from law enforcement that showed otherwise,” the statement continues. “Taylor feels solidarity with the many survivors who have endured similar hardships behind closed doors and shared only part of their stories, and she remains grateful for the outpouring of support she continues to receive. She looks forward to continuing to cooperate with the court to make progress in the custody case.”
The two had filed competing protective orders against each other, with Mortensen first filing on March 20. Hours before their April 7 hearing, Paul filed a temporary protective order against her ex.
During the April 7 hearing, Paul was granted supervised visits with her son.

The former couple's legal issues began making headlines this year after TMZ published a video on March 19 from a 2023 altercation between the two. This led to Mortensen filing his protective order and the cancellation of Paul’s then-upcoming season of “The Bachelorette.”

For the 2023 incident, Paul pled guilty in abeyance to aggravated assault, court records obtained by NBC News show. Four other charges — assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child — were dismissed.

This was followed up by a police spokesman telling NBC News on March 24 that the pair were also the subject of a 2024 domestic violence allegation after Mortensen contacted the West Jordan, Utah, Police Department. Reps for him and Paul declined to comment at the time.
